page: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities for persistent disk operations.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 232,585.66 and a PassMark CPU score of 134,134.92. Memory performance is highly competitive, registering a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 12,238.78 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 189.84 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced vector instructions, including AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI, which contribute to its strong performance in cryptographic operations and data decompression, as evidenced by a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 755.12 GB/sec.

The instance demonstrates high throughput for database and web serving workloads, achieving 178.44 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 109.94 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. It also shows capability in CPU-based machine learning, processing 7B parameter LLM prompts at 331.25 tokens per second. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for workloads requiring ultra-low latency local scratch space or hardware-accelerated deep learning. It is best suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, high-throughput web serving, database hosting, and parallel CPU-bound computing.
description: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 178.44 million operations per second. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 for accelerated cryptography and vector math. This server is highly efficient for high-throughput web serving, enterprise database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise databases and high-throughput web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput database and web workloads.
tagline: A high-core-density general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-threaded compute and memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processors
512.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or dedicated GPUs, utilizing external Premium Storage
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based inference
